summary:
Claim: The only sign not associated with hydrocephalus in neonates is "crack postsign."  
Evidence: - No known clinical condition called "crack postsign" exists; it may be a misstatement or typo. - Hydrocephalus presents with enlarged head, sunset sign, bulging fontanelle, and typically NOT depressed fontanelle.  
Answer: C

(498 tokens)

Note: Despite some confusion around fontanelle depression, the absence of any valid link between "crack postsign" and hydrocephalus—due to its nonexistence—is definitive. This makes C the best-supported exception among all options.
